title: OpenAI files for IPO as the frontier opens to the public - TCR 06/09/26
description:
OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 toward an $850bn IPO one week after Anthropic's own filing, the UK committed $1.47bn to a national AI supercomputer with homegrown chip priority, and Seattle's council weighed a data-center moratorium with Amazon's own engineers testifying for it.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-9-2026/

0:00 David Sinclair plans first human age-reversal trial
2:00 OpenAI files for IPO as both labs turn public
5:19 The data center backlash reaches Seattle and the drought map
9:08 UK and EU move to own their AI stack
12:46 Age Reversal Gets a Scoreboard
16:33 Stretchable silicon-liquid-metal robotic vision
20:24 Close

This episode covers the OpenAI IPO filing and ChatGPT superapp overhaul, the UK supercomputer and EU Open Source Strategy sovereignty push, the data-center backlash across Seattle and the drought map, David Sinclair's first human age-reversal trial, a stretchable silicon-liquid-metal imager, and Apple rebuilding Siri on Google Gemini.

The spine of the edition is one shape: the moat coming loose all at once. OpenAI and Anthropic both turned toward public markets within a single week, opening the field's revenue and liabilities to regulators and soon the public, exactly as the lead those numbers were meant to protect stops holding. The UK and EU moved to build the AI stack they used to lease, betting on domestic inference chips and open-weight models. Apple, the most integrated company on earth, sourced its flagship assistant's intelligence from a direct competitor. The data-center backlash widened to Seattle, where Amazon's own engineers asked the company to publish its water and power use, while FERC approved a tariff shifting grid risk onto the loads that create it. On the science side, David Sinclair confirmed the first human test of an oral age-reversal drug on a ten-year clock, and two Nature Materials papers pushed soft, stretchable vision and ultralow-voltage displays toward perception that conforms to living surfaces.
